source: CIS AMAZON WEB SERVICES FOUNDATIONS BENCHMARK V5.0.0
title: Ensure AWS Organizations changes are monitored

input_row.source: CIS AMAZON WEB SERVICES FOUNDATIONS BENCHMARK V5.0.0
input_row.title: Ensure AWS Organizations changes are monitored
input_row.description: Real-time monitoring of API calls can be achieved by directing CloudTrail Logs to CloudWatch Logs or an external Security Information and Event Management (SIEM) environment, and establishing corresponding metric filters and alarms. It is recommended that a metric filter and alarm be established for changes made to AWS Organizations in the master AWS account.
input_row.rationale: CloudWatch is an AWS native service that allows you to observe and monitor resources and applications. CloudTrail logs can also be sent to an external Security Information and Event Management (SIEM) environment for monitoring and alerting. Monitoring AWS Organizations changes can help you prevent unwanted, accidental, or intentional modifications that may lead to unauthorized access or other security breaches. This monitoring technique helps ensure that any unexpected changes made within your AWS Organizations can be investigated and that any unwanted changes can be rolled back.
input_row.audit: If you are using CloudTrail trails and CloudWatch, perform the following to ensure that there is at least one active multi-region CloudTrail trail with the prescribed metric filters and alarms configured: 1. Identify the log group name that is configured for use with the active multi-region CloudTrail trail: • List all CloudTrail trails: aws cloudtrail describe-trails • Identify multi-region CloudTrail trails: Trails with "IsMultiRegionTrail" set to true • Note the value associated with "Name":<trail-name> • Note the <trail-log-group-name> within the value associated with "CloudWatchLogsLogGroupArn" o Example: arn:aws:logs:<region>:<account-id>:log- group:<trail-log-group-name>:* • Ensure the identified multi-region CloudTrail trail is active: o aws cloudtrail get-trail-status --name <trail-name> ▪ Ensure IsLogging is set to TRUE • Ensure the identified multi-region CloudTrail trail captures all management events: o aws cloudtrail get-event-selectors --trail-name <trail-name> ▪ Ensure there is at least one event selector for a trail with IncludeManagementEvents set to true and ReadWriteType set to All 2. Get a list of all associated metric filters for the <trail-log-group-name> captured in step 1: aws logs describe-metric-filters --log-group-name <trail-log-group- name> 3. Ensure the output from the above command contains the following: "filterPattern": "{ ($.eventSource = organizations.amazonaws.com) && (($.eventName = "AcceptHandshake")  || ($.eventName = "AttachPolicy") || ($.eventName = "CreateAccount") || ($.eventName = "CreateOrganizationalUnit") || ($.eventName = "CreatePolicy") || ($.eventName = "DeclineHandshake") || ($.eventName = "DeleteOrganization") || ($.eventName = "DeleteOrganizationalUnit") || ($.eventName = "DeletePolicy") ||  ($.eventName = "DetachPolicy") || ($.eventName = "DisablePolicyType") || ($.eventName = "EnablePolicyType") || ($.eventName = "InviteAccountToOrganization") || ($.eventName = "LeaveOrganization") || ($.eventName = "MoveAccount") || ($.eventName = "RemoveAccountFromOrganization") || ($.eventName = "UpdatePolicy") || ($.eventName = "UpdateOrganizationalUnit")) }" 4. Note the <organizations-changes> value associated with the filterPattern from step 3. 5. Get a list of CloudWatch alarms, and filter on the <organizations-changes> captured in step 4: aws cloudwatch describe-alarms --query 'MetricAlarms[?MetricName==<organizations-changes>]' 6. Note the AlarmActions value; this will provide the SNS topic ARN value. 7. Ensure there is at least one active subscriber to the SNS topic: aws sns list-subscriptions-by-topic --topic-arn <sns-topic-arn> • At least one subscription should have "SubscriptionArn" with a valid AWS ARN. o Example of valid "SubscriptionArn": arn:aws:sns:<region>:<account- id>:<sns-topic-name>:<subscription-id>
input_row.remediation: If you are using CloudTrail trails and CloudWatch, perform the following steps to set up the metric filter, alarm, SNS topic, and subscription: 1. Create a metric filter based on the provided filter pattern that checks for AWS Organizations changes and uses the <trail-log-group-name> taken from audit step 1: aws logs put-metric-filter --log-group-name <trail-log-group-name> -- filter-name <organizations-changes> --metric-transformations metricName=<organizations- changes>,metricNamespace='CISBenchmark',metricValue=1 --filter-pattern '{ ($.eventSource = organizations.amazonaws.com) && (($.eventName = "AcceptHandshake")  || ($.eventName = "AttachPolicy")  || ($.eventName = "CreateAccount") || ($.eventName = "CreateOrganizationalUnit") || ($.eventName = "CreatePolicy") || ($.eventName = "DeclineHandshake") || ($.eventName = "DeleteOrganization") || ($.eventName = "DeleteOrganizationalUnit") || ($.eventName = "DeletePolicy") || ($.eventName = "DetachPolicy") || ($.eventName = "DisablePolicyType") || ($.eventName = "EnablePolicyType") || ($.eventName = "InviteAccountToOrganization") || ($.eventName = "LeaveOrganization") || ($.eventName = "MoveAccount") || ($.eventName = "RemoveAccountFromOrganization") || ($.eventName = "UpdatePolicy") || ($.eventName = "UpdateOrganizationalUnit")) }' Note : You can choose your own metricName and metricNamespace strings. Using the same metricNamespace for all Foundations Benchmark metrics will group them together. 2. Create an SNS topic that the alarm will notify: aws sns create-topic --name <sns-topic-name> Note : You can execute this command once and then reuse the same topic for all monitoring alarms. Note : Capture the TopicArn that is displayed when creating the SNS topic in step 2. 3. Create an SNS subscription for the topic created in step 2: aws sns subscribe --topic-arn <sns-topic-arn> --protocol <sns-protocol> --notification-endpoint <sns-subscription-endpoints> Note : You can execute this command once and then reuse the same subscription for all monitoring alarms.  4. Create an alarm that is associated with the CloudWatch Logs metric filter created in step 1 and the SNS topic created in step 2: aws cloudwatch put-metric-alarm --alarm-name <organizations-changes> -- metric-name <organizations-changes> --statistic Sum --period 300 -- threshold 1 --comparison-operator GreaterThanOrEqualToThreshold -- evaluation-periods 1 --namespace 'CISBenchmark' --alarm-actions <sns- topic-arn> References: 1. https://docs.aws.amazon.com/awscloudtrail/latest/userguide/cloudwatch-alarms- for-cloudtrail.html 2. https://docs.aws.amazon.com/organizations/latest/userguide/orgs_security_incid ent-response.html Additional Information: Configuring a log metric filter and alarm on a multi-region (global) CloudTrail trail: • ensures that activities from all regions (both used and unused) are monitored • ensures that activities on all supported global services are monitored • ensures that all management events across all regions are monitored
input_row.references: 1. https://docs.aws.amazon.com/awscloudtrail/latest/userguide/cloudwatch-alarms- for-cloudtrail.html 2. https://docs.aws.amazon.com/organizations/latest/userguide/orgs_security_incid ent-response.html Additional Information: Configuring a log metric filter and alarm on a multi-region (global) CloudTrail trail: • ensures that activities from all regions (both used and unused) are monitored • ensures that activities on all supported global services are monitored • ensures that all management events across all regions are monitored

step1_initial_assessment:
**AUDIT APPROACH:** Automated

**APPROACH DECISION:**

Based on the information provided and considering AWS's capabilities, the control can be fully verified using AWS APIs. The audit instructions outline a clear path using AWS CLI commands that can also be executed using boto3 SDK functions. This involves checking the presence and configuration of CloudTrail, CloudWatch Logs, metric filters, alarms, and SNS settings, all of which are standard AWS service properties and can be queried and configured through AWS APIs.

**PROGRAM NAME:** aws_cloudtrail_organizations_changes_monitoring_configured

**AUTOMATION DETAILS:**

To automate this compliance check, implement the following steps:

1. **Retrieve CloudTrail Trails:**
   - Use `boto3` to call `cloudtrail.describe_trails()` and identify trails with `IsMultiRegionTrail` set to `True`.
   - Check if the trail is active by using `cloudtrail.get_trail_status(Name=<trail-name>)` and confirming `IsLogging` is `True`.

2. **Verify Events Selection:**
   - Use `boto3` to call `cloudtrail.get_event_selectors(TrailName=<trail-name>)` and ensure there is at least one event selector with `IncludeManagementEvents` set to `True` and `ReadWriteType` set to `All`.

3. **Check Metric Filters:**
   - Use `boto3` to call `cloudwatchlogs.describe_metric_filters(logGroupName=<trail-log-group-name>)`.
   - Ensure a metric filter exists with the specified pattern for `organizations.amazonaws.com` events (e.g., `AcceptHandshake`, `AttachPolicy`, etc.).

4. **Verify CloudWatch Alarms:**
   - Use `boto3` to call `cloudwatch.describe_alarms()` and confirm the presence of alarms linked to the metric filters capturing AWS Organizations changes.

5. **Verify SNS Subscription:**
   - Use `boto3` to call `sns.list_subscriptions_by_topic(TopicArn=<sns-topic-arn>)`.
   - Ensure there is at least one active subscription with a valid `SubscriptionArn`.

**Pass/Fail Criteria:**
- **Pass:** If a multi-region CloudTrail is active, configured to capture management events, with appropriate metric filters and alarms for AWS Organizations changes, and there is at least one active SNS subscription.
- **Fail:** If any of the above configurations are missing or incorrect. 

By utilizing AWS APIs, you can automate the checking of these configurations, ensuring continuous compliance monitoring for AWS Organizations changes.
step2_review:
**APPROACH VALIDATION:** AGREE

- **Manual vs Automated Classification:** The classification as Automated is correct. The control can be fully verified using AWS APIs, specifically through boto3 and AWS CLI. The steps outlined leverage AWS's capabilities to query and verify configurations programmatically.
- **AWS SDK Capabilities:** The use of boto3 methods to interact with CloudTrail, CloudWatch, and SNS is appropriate and aligns with AWS SDK capabilities. These services provide comprehensive APIs to automate the compliance check.

**TECHNICAL ACCURACY:** 8

- **Automation Details:** The automation details are mostly accurate. The boto3 methods mentioned are valid for retrieving and verifying the necessary configurations. However, there is a minor oversight in ensuring that the log group associated with the CloudTrail is correctly identified and that the metric filter pattern is comprehensive enough to capture all relevant AWS Organizations changes.
- **Program Name:** The program name `aws_cloudtrail_organizations_changes_monitoring_configured` is somewhat descriptive but could be more precise. It should reflect the specific security check more clearly, such as including terms like `metric_filters` or `alarms`.

**IMPROVEMENTS:**

- **Log Group Identification:** Ensure that the log group associated with the CloudTrail is correctly identified, as this is crucial for verifying metric filters.
- **Metric Filter Pattern:** The pattern for metric filters should be comprehensive and cover all relevant AWS Organizations API actions. Consider specifying the exact pattern or providing a list of critical actions.
- **Program Name Suggestion:** Consider renaming the program to `aws_cloudtrail_organizations_metric_filters_alarms_configured` to better reflect the specific checks being performed.
- **Additional Considerations:** Ensure that the CloudTrail is configured to deliver logs to CloudWatch Logs, as this is a prerequisite for setting up metric filters and alarms.

**CONFIDENCE:** HIGH

- The assessment is well-founded, and the use of AWS APIs is appropriate for automating this compliance check. The technical details are mostly accurate, with minor areas for improvement.

**FINAL RECOMMENDATION:** Modify

- **Modifications Needed:**
  - Ensure the log group associated with CloudTrail is correctly identified for metric filter verification.
  - Specify a comprehensive pattern for metric filters to capture all relevant AWS Organizations changes.
  - Update the program name to be more descriptive of the specific security check being performed.

These modifications will enhance the accuracy and clarity of the compliance check, ensuring it effectively monitors AWS Organizations changes.

step3_final_decision.decision_text:
**FINAL APPROACH:** Automated

**JUSTIFICATION:**  
Both AI opinions agree that this control can be fully verified using AWS APIs, specifically through boto3 and AWS CLI. All required properties—CloudTrail multi-region status, log group association, metric filters, and alarms—are accessible and auditable via AWS SDKs. No manual inspection or console-only verification is necessary, as all configuration elements are exposed and can be programmatically validated.

**CONFIDENCE:** HIGH

---

**PROGRAM NAME:**  
aws_cloudtrail_log_group_organizations_changes_metric_filter_and_alarm_configured

**AUTOMATION DETAILS:**
- **AWS Resource/Service:** CloudTrail Trails, CloudWatch Log Groups, CloudWatch Metric Filters, CloudWatch Alarms, SNS Topics
- **API/CLI Method:**  
  - `boto3.client('cloudtrail').describe_trails()`  
  - `boto3.client('cloudtrail').get_trail_status(Name=<trail-name>)`  
  - `boto3.client('cloudtrail').get_event_selectors(TrailName=<trail-name>)`  
  - `boto3.client('logs').describe_log_groups()`  
  - `boto3.client('logs').describe_metric_filters(logGroupName=<log-group-name>)`  
  - `boto3.client('cloudwatch').describe_alarms()`  
  - `boto3.client('sns').list_subscriptions_by_topic(TopicArn=<sns-topic-arn>)`
- **Properties to Check:**  
  - CloudTrail trail is multi-region (`IsMultiRegionTrail: True`) and logging (`IsLogging: True`)
  - Trail has management events enabled (`IncludeManagementEvents: True`)
  - Trail is associated with a CloudWatch Log Group
  - Log Group has a metric filter for AWS Organizations changes (filter pattern includes `organizations.amazonaws.com`)
  - CloudWatch Alarm exists for the metric filter and is configured to notify an SNS topic
  - SNS topic has at least one valid subscription
- **Pass Criteria:**  
  - At least one active, multi-region CloudTrail trail with management events enabled and logging to a CloudWatch Log Group
  - The log group has a metric filter matching AWS Organizations API calls (e.g., `AcceptHandshake`, `AttachPolicy`, etc.)
  - A CloudWatch Alarm is set on the metric and notifies an SNS topic with active subscriptions
- **Fail Criteria:**  
  - No active multi-region CloudTrail trail with management events and logging enabled
  - No metric filter for AWS Organizations changes on the log group
  - No CloudWatch Alarm or SNS notification configured for the metric filter

---

**DISAGREEMENTS RESOLVED:**  
Both AI opinions agreed on the automated approach. The second opinion suggested a more descriptive program name and emphasized the importance of correctly identifying the log group and comprehensive metric filter patterns. These points are incorporated in the final program name and automation details for maximum clarity and precision.

**SOURCE:** CIS AMAZON WEB SERVICES FOUNDATIONS BENCHMARK V5.0.0
